articles.title: IMD issues five day heat wave alert for TG - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
IMD issues five day heat wave alert for TG
Hyderabad: The IMD has sounded a heatwave alert in Telangana for the next five days, with temperatures expected to rise above 40 degrees across the state. North Telangana districts reported temperatures exceeding 42 degrees on Tuesday.
As a precaution, the state government appealed to the people not to venture out of their houses in the afternoon. IMD officials said Nizamabad registered the highest reading at 42.4 degrees Celsius. Temperatures of 42.3 degrees were recorded in Adilabad, and 41 degrees in Mahabubnagar.
Hyderabad is also witnessing temperatures exceeding 40 degrees. In many parts of the state, levels are currently hovering two to three degrees above normal. The intensity of the heat begins to rise early in the morning, reaching its peak during the afternoon hours.
Meteorological officials indicated this situation is likely to persist and advised the public to refrain from venturing outdoors unless absolutely necessary. State Panchayat Raj Minister Sitakka appealed to citizens to avoid outdoor activities during peak hours and directed officials to set up drinking water kiosks in major Gram Panchayats and mandal headquarters. In unavoidable situations, she said, people should cover their heads with caps or towels to protect themselves.
She cautioned that failure to take precautions could lead to sunstroke. The Minister also addressed Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme workers, acknowledging their challenges during summer.
She noted that while employment guarantee works are largely undertaken now, workers must take adequate precautions, including wearing protective headgear and ensuring access to drinking water at worksites to ensure their health and safety remains protected throughout this severe weather period across the region.

articles.title: Heatwave in Telangana with temperatures above 40°C - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Telangana is experiencing intense heatwave conditions with maximum temperatures exceeding 40°C in several areas over the past two days, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD) Scientist Dharmaraju. He stated that dry weather conditions are prevailing across the state and temperatures are expected to increase further in the coming days.
“For the past two days, Telangana State has been experiencing maximum temperatures above 40°C. Dry weather conditions are currently prevailing over the state,” Dharmaraju said.
He noted that several districts have recorded high temperatures, including Adilabad at around 41°C and Nizamabad at 41.4°C. Cities such as Medak, Hanumakonda, Khammam, and Mahabubnagar also reported temperatures near or above 40°C. “The city recorded a maximum temperature of 40.1°C yesterday,” he added, warning that temperatures may rise by 2°C to 3°C over the next five days.
The IMD morning bulletin predicted changing weather patterns across various parts of the country. A hot and humid weather warning was issued for multiple regions, including isolated pockets of Telangana, Rayalaseema, Madhya Maharashtra, North Interior Karnataka, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Rajasthan, and Jharkhand between April 15 and 20, with differing peak periods.
The IMD also forecast likely hot and humid conditions in isolated areas of Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha during April 15-20, and in coastal Maharashtra, coastal Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, coastal Andhra Pradesh, and coastal Karnataka during intervals between April 15 and 18. Authorities advised the public to remain cautious amid rising temperatures and uncomfortable weather.
In Northeast India, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is expected over Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura between April 15 and 18, with heavy rainfall in some locations during this time.
Northwest India is forecast to receive rainfall and snowfall in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana, along with thunderstorms, strong winds, and hailstorms in certain areas of Jammu and Kashmir.
East India anticipates scattered rainfall with thunderstorms in West Bengal, Sikkim, and Odisha at various times from April 15 to 20. In South India, light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and gusty winds is expected over Interior and Coastal Karnataka between April 15 and 18.
The IMD advised people to remain alert as weather conditions may vary across regions.

articles.title: Telangana faces intense heat, mercury crosses 43°C in many areas - Hyderabad Mail
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: Heat conditions intensified across Telangana on Tuesday, with several districts recording temperatures above 43°C, while Hyderabad continued to remain above the 40°C mark, accompanied by warmer nights.
According to the Telangana State Development Planning Society (TSDPS), the highest temperature of 43.5°C was recorded at Adavi Devula Palli in Nalgonda district. Jagtial followed closely at 43.4°C, while Nirmal and Nizamabad reported 43.3°C each.
Adilabad, Karimnagar, Mancherial and Kamareddy recorded temperatures between 42.8°C and 43.2°C, indicating a steady rise across north and central Telangana.
Urban heat adds to discomfort after sunset hours
Hyderabad recorded 40.9°C at Saidabad, with Begumpet, Mehdipatnam and Jubilee Hills at around 40.8°C. Uppal, Kapra and Kukatpally crossed 40.4°C.
The state’s average maximum temperature stood at 41.1°C. While slightly lower than last year’s peak of 43.4°C, the impact remains widespread.
Nights have also turned warmer, offering little relief. Residents reported discomfort even after sunset, with retained urban heat worsening conditions.
Street vendors, delivery workers and two-wheeler riders said routine work has become more difficult, with reduced public movement during peak afternoon hours.
IMD forecasts continued heatwave conditions statewide
With temperatures already crossing 43°C in several districts, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned of a further rise of 2–3°C.
The forecast indicates persistence of heatwave conditions in the coming days, raising concerns over prolonged exposure and public health risks.

articles.title: Telangana Heatwave Worsens as Temperatures Cross 43 Degrees Officials Sound Alert - The South India Times
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Severe heatwave grips Telangana with temperatures crossing 43 degrees officials warn of further rise advise people to stay indoors take precautions and avoid exposure during peak daytime hours
Telangana heatwave drives temperatures above 43 degrees across districts
Telangana heatwave alert issued as temperatures may touch 45 degrees
Telangana heatwave safety advisory urges people to avoid daytime exposure
A strong heatwave has tightened its grip over Telangana, with temperatures rising sharply across several districts and creating harsh living conditions for residents. The intensity of heat usually seen in peak summer months has already arrived, leaving people concerned about the coming weeks.
Weather data indicates that multiple regions have recorded temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius, with the highest touching 43.5 degrees in parts of Nalgonda district. Other areas including Jagtial Nirmal Nizamabad Adilabad and Karimnagar have also reported similarly high temperatures, reflecting a widespread rise across the state.
The impact of the heat is being felt from early morning, with temperatures climbing rapidly by mid morning and becoming extreme by afternoon. Streets are largely deserted during peak hours as people avoid stepping out unless necessary. Many households are depending heavily on cooling appliances to cope with the conditions.
Meteorological officials have warned that the heatwave is likely to intensify further over the next few days. Forecasts suggest that temperatures could rise beyond 45 degrees Celsius in some areas, increasing the risk of heat related illnesses.

articles.title: IMD warns of intense heatwave in Telangana as mercury crosses 40°C - Asianet Newsable
articles.full_text:
Telangana is witnessing intense heatwave conditions, with maximum temperatures crossing 40°C in several parts of the state over the past two days, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) Scientist Dharmaraju said. Speaking to ANI, Dharmaraju said that dry weather conditions are prevailing across the state, with temperatures expected to rise further in the coming days.
"For the past two days, Telangana State has been experiencing maximum temperatures above 40°C. Dry weather conditions are currently prevailing over the state," he said. He added that several districts recorded high temperatures, with Adilabad reaching around 41°C and Nizamabad recording 41.4°C. Cities like Medak, Hanumakonda, Khammam, and Mahabubnagar also recorded temperatures around or above 40°C.
"The city recorded a maximum temperature of 40.1°C yesterday," he said, adding that temperatures may increase by 2°C to 3°C over the next five days.
The IMD morning bulletin forecast changing weather conditions across several parts of the country. It issued a hot and humid weather warning for several parts of the country, saying that heatwave conditions are very likely in isolated pockets of Telangana, Rayalaseema, Madhya Maharashtra, North Interior Karnataka, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Rajasthan, and Jharkhand between April 15 and 20, with varying peak periods across these regions.
It added that hot and humid conditions are also very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha during April 15-20, along with coastal Maharashtra, coastal Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, coastal Andhra Pradesh and coastal Karnataka during different periods between April 15 and 18, advising people to remain cautious amid rising temperatures and uncomfortable weather conditions.
Meanwhile, in Northeast India, IMD predicted light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is likely over Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura between April 15 and 18. Heavy rainfall is also likely in some areas during this period.
In Northwest India, rainfall and snowfall are expected over Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana, with thunderstorms and strong winds. Hailstorms are also likely in parts of Jammu & Kashmir.
East India anticipates scattered rainfall with thunderstorms over West Bengal, Sikkim, and Odisha at various phases from April 15 to 20.
In South India, light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and gusty winds is likely over Interior and Coastal Karnataka between April 15 and 18.
The IMD has advised people to stay alert as weather conditions may vary across regions. (ANI)
